seo.title: diBoaS Investors | Money With a Purpose. Cash With Rules.
seo.description: diBoaS is a Berlin-based pre-seed fintech building a non-custodial financial app that helps consumers assign money to goals and businesses assign cash to rules before money moves.
seo.ogTitle: Money should know what it is there to do.
seo.ogDescription: A non-custodial control layer for money before it moves. Pre-seed, Berlin. Risks named directly.

hero.headline: Money should know what it is there to do.
hero.subheadline: diBoaS is building a non-custodial financial app that helps consumers assign money to goals and businesses assign cash to rules before money moves.
hero.supporting: Before funds move, diBoaS shows the strategy, cost, liquidity, and risk. Users stay in control. diBoaS is the interface, never the holder.

thesis.label: THE THESIS
thesis.headline: B2C builds trust. B2B builds the business.
body: Consumer fintech looks large from the outside and thin in the unit economics.
diBoaS does not pretend otherwise.
The consumer product builds trust, brand, and distribution. The business product drives revenue through cash rules, payment paths, liquidity control, and explicit movement fees.
That is the company thesis: B2C builds trust and distribution. B2B drives revenue.
thesis.pullQuote: The model is not built on hiding consumer ARPU. It is built on being honest about it.

building.label: WHAT WE ARE BUILDING
building.headline: A control layer for money before it moves.
body: Most financial interfaces show money as one balance. But money has different jobs.
For a consumer, it may be an emergency reserve, a future purchase, or protection from inflation.
For a business, it may be payroll, rent, taxes, supplier payments, operating reserves, or excess liquidity.
diBoaS starts with the job of the money. Then it shows the available path, cost, liquidity, and risk before action.
building.productLine: Consumers choose a goal. Businesses set rules. diBoaS shows the path before money moves.

whyNow.label: WHY NOW
whyNow.headline: The rails changed. The interface did not.
body: Money can now move, settle, and be programmed in ways that were not available to ordinary users or small businesses a decade ago. But most products still speak to experts.
Consumers are offered promises instead of explanations. Businesses manage cash through banks, cards, spreadsheets, payment tools, and reports that rarely fit together.
diBoaS is built around a simpler standard: before money moves, the user should understand the path, cost, liquidity, and risk.

marketSequence.label: MARKET SEQUENCE
marketSequence.headline: One product spine. Four market-native propositions.
marketSequence.body: diBoaS is designed for four launch markets, each with a different cultural and financial entry point.
markets: United States: money with a job, access, and movement.
Germany: planning, clarity, and visible risk.
Spain: plan, costs, prudence, and explanation.
marketSequence.note: The product spine stays consistent: purpose, rules, cost, liquidity, risk, and user control. The market story changes because trust is local.

businessModel.label: BUSINESS MODEL
businessModel.headline: Revenue follows movement, not hidden spread.
body: diBoaS earns through explicit movement fees.
The company does not take a spread on strategy returns. Revenue scales when money enters, exits, or a strategy closes.
The honest model is simple: consumer ARPU is structurally small. B2C builds trust and distribution. B2B drives revenue.
businessModel.supporting: A small number of business clients can be worth more than thousands of individual users.

status.label: CURRENT STATUS
status.headline: Early, specified, and honest about it.
body: diBoaS is pre-launch and pre-seed.
The marketing site and market-intelligence product are live. The money product is specified and planned for the funded build.
The current raise funds the money-product build, regulatory launch gates, security audit, infrastructure, incorporation, and first B2B validation.
items: Money product: specified, not yet built

founder.label: FOUNDER
founder.headline: Built by a product operator, not a campaign.
body: diBoaS is based in Berlin and led by a solo founder with 20 years of product and IT experience across Brazil, the United States, Japan, and Germany.

The founder has built the current product foundation, research, architecture, regulatory analysis, financial model, and investor materials with an AI-assisted build process and a test-driven operating standard.
The financing supports the next phase of the build: product development, technical support, legal review, security work, infrastructure, incorporation, and early B2B validation.

raise.label: THE RAISE
raise.headline: Raising to prove the right thing.
body: diBoaS is preparing a pre-seed raise to fund the money-product build, regulatory launch gates, security audit, infrastructure, incorporation, and first B2B validation.
The goal of the raise is not scale theater. It is proof: build the money product, clear the launch gates, validate the first B2B demand, and turn assumptions into evidence.
raise.privateNote: Detailed raise structure is shared in the private investor materials.

materials.label: INVESTOR MATERIALS
materials.headline: The full answers are available on request.
body: The investor room includes the Business Plan, Investor FAQ, and supporting materials covering the model, risks, use of funds, regulatory position, product roadmap, fee logic, and technical architecture.
The FAQ is intentionally direct. If something is unresolved, it is named.

request.title: Request investor materials
request.intro: Tell us who you are and we will share the appropriate investor materials.
request.success: Thanks. We will review your request and follow up with the appropriate materials.
request.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
request.privacyNote: We use your details only to review your request and follow up. Your information is encrypted.

page.riskNote: diBoaS is pre-launch. The money product is not yet available. diBoaS is designed as a non-custodial interface and is not a bank, deposit account, savings account, or guarantee. Product availability depends on legal review, partner availability, and launch gates.

seo.title: diBoaS Investor Room
seo.description: Private investor materials for diBoaS, a Berlin-based pre-seed fintech building a non-custodial financial app for consumer goals and business cash rules.

room.accessNote: Private investor materials. Do not distribute without permission.

hero.headline: Start with the hard questions.
hero.subheadline: This room contains the current pre-seed materials for diBoaS: the Business Plan, Investor FAQ, use of funds, revenue logic, regulatory position, product roadmap, fee logic, and technical architecture.
hero.supporting: diBoaS is pre-launch and pre-seed. Numbers are founder assumptions unless stated otherwise. Open risks are named directly.

startHere.headline: Start here.
body: If you only read two documents, start with the Business Plan and Investor FAQ.
The Business Plan gives the one-page view.
The Investor FAQ gives the uncomfortable answers first: business model, unit economics, regulatory assumptions, open risks, solo-founder risk, use of funds, and what still needs to be proven.

materials.headline: Current materials
materials.intro: The documents below are organized from overview to deeper diligence.

docs.title: Business Plan
docs.summary: One-page investor overview: product, problem, model, market sequence, go-to-market, raise paths, and key model risks.

docs.title: Investor FAQ
docs.summary: Detailed investor Q&A with blunt answers on unit economics, regulation, custody, traction, moat, team, deal structure, and unresolved risks.

docs.title: Pitch Deck
docs.summary: Short narrative deck for first conversations.

docs.title: Use of Funds
docs.summary: What the lean path funds, what the larger path buys down, and what milestones the capital is meant to prove.

docs.title: Revenue Model Summary
docs.summary: Consumer ARPU, B2B ARPU, break-even logic, first metrics to validate, and what breaks the model.

docs.title: Regulatory Position Summary
docs.summary: Non-custodial architecture, jurisdiction-specific gates, EU risk, Brazil partner dependency, and US user-to-user transfer opinion.

docs.title: Product Roadmap
docs.summary: What is live, what is specified, what the raise funds, and what comes after launch.

docs.title: Fees Summary
docs.summary: Canonical fee structure, fee caps, pass-through costs, and where diBoaS charges $0.

docs.title: Technical Architecture Summary
docs.summary: Wallet architecture, provider dependencies, non-custodial flow, recovery model, and technical risk areas.

rows.label: Business wedge
rows.value: Cash rules, payment paths, liquidity control

rows.label: Current status
rows.value: Marketing site and market-intelligence product live; money product specified

rows.label: Raise purpose
rows.value: Money-product build, regulatory gates, audit, infrastructure, incorporation, first B2B validation

builtNotBuilt.headline: What is built, what is not.
builtNotBuilt.closing: That is the point of the raise.

raiseProves.headline: The raise funds proof, not theater.
body: The first money should prove the right things:

Can the money product ship safely? Can the regulatory launch gates be cleared? Can a money-in/out partner be signed? Can early users fund accounts? Can B2B demand show up at the assumed deal sizes? Can the founder-built plan hold, or should the senior engineer path be activated earlier?

openRisks.headline: Open risks are named.
openRisks.intro: The main unresolved items are not hidden:
items: Exact net margin not yet pinned.
US user-to-user transfer opinion still required.
openRisks.closing: Each one has a gate. None is treated as solved.

regulatory.headline: Regulation is treated as governed risk.
body: diBoaS is designed as a non-custodial interface. It does not hold customer money, does not hold customer keys, and charges usage fees for software.

The correct framing is not "no license required." Based on the current regulatory memo, that architecture is designed to reduce licensing triggers, subject to jurisdiction-specific legal review and launch gates in the US, EU, and Brazil. This is a statement of design intent, not a legal opinion.
The full regulatory summary explains the US, EU, and Brazil dependencies.

contact.headline: Questions, objections, or follow-up.
contact.body: If you are reviewing the materials and want to discuss the model, risks, or raise structure, reach out directly.

room.footerNote: These materials are provided for discussion purposes only. They include forward-looking assumptions, unresolved risks, and pre-launch estimates. They are not legal, tax, financial, accounting, or investment advice. Private investor materials. Please do not forward or distribute without permission.

docPage.inPrepBody: This summary is in preparation and will be added to the room shortly. In the meantime, reach out and we will share the current draft directly.
